name: tokei (Fast Code Statistics)

performance.notes: Rust-based; much faster than cloc; parallel file processing

agent_readiness.notes: Lines of code counter. Fast Rust-based alternative to cloc. tokei command shows per-language breakdown: lines, blanks, comments, code. 200+ languages. --output json/cbor/yaml/toml. --sort. --exclude. .tokeignore for exclusions. Respects .gitignore. Used in CI for codebase size tracking. GitHub badges via shields.io tokei integration. scc (Sloc Cloc and Code) is similar competitor. cloc is older Python alternative.

gotchas: CLI tool only — not a library
Comment counting accuracy varies by language
Not a substitute for static analysis
best_when: Codebase size reporting in CI/CD or README
Language composition analysis for multi-language projects
Technical debt estimation (lines of code baseline)
GitHub README badges showing code size
avoid_when: Code quality analysis (use SonarQube or static analyzers)
Programmatic use in code (invoke via subprocess)
summary: tokei counts lines of code with per-language breakdown. Rust-based, much faster than cloc. 200+ languages, --output json for CI integration. Respects .gitignore. GitHub badge integration via shields.io. Best for codebase size reporting, CI metrics, and README badges. scc is a similar alternative.
